name: FLEX
description: A preset-driven synthesizer combining subtractive, wavetable, multisample, FM and AM engines, played through a fixed set of designer-defined macros and a master FX chain (filter, delay, reverb, limiter).

name: Sytrus
description: An advanced hybrid synthesizer built on six operators with FM, ring modulation and subtractive synthesis, routed through a modulation matrix, per-operator envelopes, three filters and built-in effects.

name: Fruity Parametric EQ 2
description: A 7-band parametric equalizer with adjustable frequency, gain and bandwidth per band, multiple filter types and slopes, optional high-quality and linear-phase modes, and a spectral analyzer for precise tonal shaping.

name: FPC
description: A pad-based drum sampler with an MPC-inspired layout. Each pad stacks multiple sample layers with velocity ranges (for automatic ghost notes and round-robins), custom ADSR per layer, a pattern manager for loading MIDI loops, and independent mixer routing per pad.

name: Fruity Blood Overdrive
description: An aggressive overdrive/distortion. A pre-amp control sets input drive into the clipping stage, a Color control is a bandpass filter that focuses which frequencies are distorted, and post-gain sets the output level. It excels at harsh, buzzy bass and lead tones.

name: Fruity Compressor
description: A straightforward single-band compressor for everyday dynamics control. Threshold and ratio set when and how much it compresses, selectable knee types (Hard, Medium, Soft and Vintage) change the shape of the compression curve, and gain makeup restores level after reduction.

name: Fruity Convolver
description: A convolution reverb and linear-phase processor. It applies impulse responses (halls, rooms, plates) in real time at low latency, includes a spectral linear-phase EQ that shapes the reverb or dry signal without phase shift, can stretch the impulse's length and pitch, and shapes the tail's volume, pan and stereo width with envelopes.

name: Fruity Delay 3
description: A tape- and analog-style delay. Changing the delay time slides pitch like a varispeed tape machine; it adds distortion and saturation to echoes, high- and low-pass filtering (bandpass) in the feedback path, wow-and-flutter modulation, diffusion that smears transients into ambient textures, and BPM sync that holds time through tempo automation.

name: Fruity DX10
description: An approachable 2-operator-style FM synthesizer. Simple Coarse and Fine controls set the modulation ratio, a master Wave Brightness control scales overall harmonic richness, and a built-in vibrato LFO adds organic pitch movement - a fast route to FM bells, e-pianos and basses.

name: Fruity Filter
description: A lightweight state-variable filter for quick tone shaping. It offers low-pass, high-pass and band-pass types with a resonance control to emphasise the cutoff, and 2x oversampling to reduce aliasing during fast cutoff automation, at a very low CPU cost.

name: Fruity Granulizer
description: A granular synthesizer and sampler that plays a sample back as a stream of small grains. Grain Attack, Hold and Spacing controls reshape the texture; because playback position and pitch are decoupled, you can time-stretch a sample down toward 1 percent speed while holding pitch, or pitch-shift without changing duration.

name: Fruity Limiter
description: A dual-stage dynamics processor: a compressor stage feeds a limiter stage, with a separate noise gate. It provides a dedicated sidechain input for pumping effects, lookahead in the limiter to catch transients, and visual feedback of input, output and gain reduction.

name: Fruity Love Philter
description: A multi-unit filter bank with deep modulation. Each filter unit has hand-drawn envelopes and LFO shapes, a built-in waveshaper for per-unit distortion, and an X/Y controller for real-time performance. A pattern sequencer can trigger different envelope patterns from the piano roll for rhythmic, gated filtering.

name: Fruity Reeverb 2
description: An algorithmic reverb modelling acoustic spaces from rooms to halls and cathedrals. It offers mid/side processing to widen the stereo edges without muddying the centre, a bass multiplier to set low-frequency decay independently of the highs, and tempo-synced LFO modulation of the reverb time for organic movement.

name: Fruity Slicer
description: A simple beat slicer and sampler. It detects transients to cut a loop into slices you can play from the keyboard, time-stretches the sample to match project BPM automatically, and dumps the sliced groove to the piano roll as a note sequence for re-arranging.

name: Fruity Vocoder
description: A zero-latency vocoder and filter bank. It imposes a modulator's spectrum (usually a voice) onto a carrier in real time, making it suitable for live performance and tracking. Formant shifting changes vocal character, and a hold function freezes the modulator's spectral state for sustained pads.

name: Fruity WaveShaper
description: A waveshaping distortion driven by a hand-drawn transfer-function graph. A spline curve maps input amplitude to output for anything from gentle saturation to hard clipping; unipolar/bipolar modes process the full wave or the positive and negative halves independently, and oversampling reduces aliasing on extreme shapes.

name: Gross Beat
description: A time-and-volume shaper built on a continuously recording 2-bar rolling buffer. Time envelopes manipulate playback position for stutters, scratches and reverse; volume envelopes gate and swell the signal. Envelopes are recalled from 36 time and 36 volume slots triggered by MIDI notes.

name: Harmless
description: A synthesizer that applies subtractive-style filtering and effects to an additive harmonic core before the sound is rendered. A single-page, colour-coded interface exposes harmonic masking (which partials are allowed to sound), a specialized pluck filter that models string decay, and an unusual phaser that can shift pitch and create resonant shapes.

name: Harmor
description: An additive synthesizer and resynthesizer that builds each voice from up to 516 sine partials across two independent engines (A and B). It can resynthesize imported audio and images into editable additive data, with pitch-independent time-stretch and formant shift, plus signature Prism, Pluck, Blur and Strum processors and hand-drawn filter shapes.

name: Maximus
description: A multiband dynamics processor and maximizer. It splits the signal into Low, Mid and High bands plus a Master stage, each shaped by a user-drawn spline compression/expansion curve with per-band pre/post gain, adaptive release, and optional multiband saturation.

name: Morphine
description: An additive synthesizer that builds each voice from up to 128 harmonics, with harmonic breakpoints for evolving spectra, sample resynthesis that imports WAV/AIFF into editable harmonics, 128 keyboard zones for layering different sounds, specialized PWM spectral filters for unison and width, and a noise generator for realistic attacks and grit.

name: Newtone
description: A pitch-correction and melody editor. It analyses monophonic audio into editable notes you can drag to new pitches with snap-to-grid, redraw the pitch contour to tame vibrato and drift, shift formants independently of pitch, and smooth transitions between notes. Global Center and Variation controls apply quick overall corrections.

name: Patcher
description: A modular routing environment that hosts other generators and effects on a visual patch canvas. You wire nodes to define audio and MIDI signal flow, chain any number of plugins inside one instance, split signals by frequency or amplitude for multiband processing, and design a custom control surface (knobs, sliders, XY pads) that exposes the whole patch as a single instrument or effect.

name: PoiZone
description: A subtractive synthesizer built for trance and analog styles. It pairs a 4-voice unison engine (stereo spread and detune) with a 16-step trance gate for instant rhythmic movement, a full arpeggiator, and built-in delay, chorus and reverb effects.

name: Sawer
description: A vintage-styled subtractive synth modelled on the Polivoks character. It pairs a 12/24 dB LP/BP/HP Polivoks-style filter with saw, sub and noise oscillators, hard sync for tearing leads, 8-voice unison for width, a modulation matrix for LFO and envelope routing, and vintage chorus, phaser, delay and reverb effects.

name: Slicex
description: A beat slicer and sampler that auto-detects transients to cut a loop into playable slices, each editable in a built-in wave editor. It provides dual decks for layering or crossfading two loops, eight articulation slots (filters and envelopes) assignable to slices, and one-click dump of the sliced groove to the piano roll as MIDI.

name: Soundgoodizer
description: A one-knob stereo maximizer and multiband exciter powered by the Maximus engine. A single amount knob blends the processing from 0 to 100 percent, and four character modes (A-D) preset different multiband curves for instant loudness and polish at negligible CPU cost.

name: Toxic Biohazard
description: An FM and subtractive hybrid synthesizer. An FM matrix routes operators into one another, feeding an analog-modelled LP/BP/HP filter with its own envelope, 8-voice stereo unison, and an integrated FX rack (multi-tap delay, chorus, phaser, reverb, EQ and distortion). Custom waveforms can be loaded from WAV/AIFF files.

name: Vocodex
description: A vocoder that imposes the spectral character of a modulator (typically a voice) onto a carrier signal. It includes a built-in Sytrus-based internal carrier so no external synth is required, band contouring for speech intelligibility, band unison for thickness, an envelope follower for hold/release control, and a built-in Soundgoodizer maximizer on the output.

name: 3x Osc
description: FL Studio's classic three-oscillator subtractive synthesizer and default instrument. Three independently tunable oscillators (selectable waveforms, detune and phase) feed a filter and envelope, making it a common starting point for simple analog-style basses and leads.

name: BooBass
description: A minimal bass synthesizer with three tone controls (bass, mid and high) for quickly dialling a simple, usable bassline. Built for speed rather than deep editing.

name: BassDrum
description: A drum synthesizer for building kick and percussion tones by synthesis rather than samples, shaping a tuned body together with attack and click components.

name: Fruit Kick
description: A dedicated kick-drum synthesizer built on a sine sweep from a high start frequency down to a low end frequency, with a click generator for transient attack and cut-through.

name: Fruity Balance
description: A lightweight volume and pan utility for automating level and balance without touching the mixer faders.

name: Fruity Chorus
description: A chorus that layers slightly delayed, detuned copies of the signal. Delay sets the voice offset (about 2-20 ms), depth sets the pitch-modulation amount, and a stereo control widens the effect via phase.

name: Fruity Delay
description: A simple echo line using time-based (ms/step) delay, with a feedback control for the number of repeats and a low-pass filter in the feedback path to darken echoes over time.

name: Fruity Delay 2
description: A digital stereo delay with feedback filtering (echoes get duller over time), stereo offset for Haas-style width, and tempo sync to project BPM steps.

name: Fruity Fast Dist
description: A quick distortion and saturation with a pre-amp drive, a threshold control that also sets frequency sensitivity, and an A/B switch offering two flavours from calm to nasty.

name: Fruity Flanger
description: A flanger that mixes the signal with a very short, modulated delayed copy to create sweeping comb-filter notches. Feedback intensifies the metallic resonance; inverting the feedback gives a hollower tone.

name: Fruity Phaser
description: An all-pass phaser that creates moving notches in the spectrum. The number of stages sets how many notches (intensity), feedback sharpens the resonant peaks, and sweep range sets how far the notches travel.

name: Fruity Free Filter
description: A single-band multimode filter with selectable modes, a Q/resonance control for peak sharpness, and gain for EQ-style boost or cut, tuned for smooth automation.

name: Fruity Fast LP
description: A low-CPU low-pass filter optimized for fast LFO and envelope modulation without zipper noise, with aggressive resonance capable of self-oscillation for acid-style squelches.

name: Fruity Soft Clipper
description: A soft clipper that rounds peaks with musical harmonic saturation, letting signals push past 0 dB for extra loudness and gluing transient-heavy material like drums.

name: Fruity Parametric EQ
description: The original parametric equalizer with multiple per-band filter types (peaking, shelf, low/high-pass, band-pass, notch), vertical gain sliders and frequency/Q knobs. The lighter predecessor to Parametric EQ 2.

name: Fruity Reeverb
description: The original algorithmic reverb with room-size and decay-time controls for classic hall and room tails; the lighter predecessor to Reeverb 2.

name: Fruity 7 Band EQ
description: A simple graphic equalizer with seven fixed bands and instant visual feedback of the curve, for quick broad-stroke tone shaping at low CPU cost.

name: Fruity Bass Boost
description: A single-purpose bass enhancer that boosts a target low frequency by a set amount for quick low-end lift.

name: Fruity Stereo Enhancer
description: A stereo width and phase utility that raises side level relative to mid, can offset one channel by up to 500 ms for psychoacoustic width, and adds pan and phase-inversion controls.

name: Fruity Stereo Shaper
description: A stereo matrix processor with four sliders defining the relationship between left and right channels, enabling mid/side separation, channel swapping and width control.

name: Fruity Phase Inverter
description: A minimal phase utility that flips the polarity of the left and/or right channel - useful for fixing phase cancellation or checking mono compatibility.

name: Fruity Squeeze
description: A bit-crusher and lo-fi distortion. A squarize control reduces bit depth for digital grit and stepped artifacts, while a puncher section replaces samples with distorted nominal-level versions for aggressive lo-fi textures.

name: Vintage Chorus
description: An analog chorus modelling a bucket-brigade (BBD) delay line, with classic I, II and I+II modes for authentic vintage ensemble textures.

name: Vintage Phaser
description: An analog phaser emulation in the style of the 1970s Small Stone circuit, with a dual-LFO system (separate L/R LFOs with phase offset) for wide, swirling sweeps.

name: Hyper Chorus
description: A multi-tap chorus and ensemble with three X/Y pads controlling delay/feedback, modulation and movement, plus built-in auto-panning for spatial motion inside the chorus algorithm.

name: Multiband Delay
description: A multiband delay that splits the signal into bands, each with its own delay (0-100 ms), volume and pan, plus a master Scale knob that extends the range up to 1000 ms or reverses it for spectral delay effects.

name: Fruity Flangus
description: A multi-voice flanger and unison simulator that runs several flangers in parallel (Order) with stereo spread across the field for thick, chorus-like widening.

name: Pitch Shifter
description: A pitch shifter and harmonizer with a monophonic Voice mode (formant control for natural vocal shifts) and a polyphonic granular Music mode for chords and wide intervals.

name: GMS
description: A hybrid wavetable synthesizer with three independent oscillators using custom waveshapes. It supports FM, ring-mod, oscillator sync and standard subtractive modes, with an advanced unison engine for thick, detuned, stereo-spread textures.

name: Sakura
description: A physical-modelling synthesizer for string and plucked tones. Two string engines layer, driven by exciters (pluck, bow, scrape, hammer or noise) and shaped by a body resonator that simulates wood, metal or box size.

name: Kepler
description: A subtractive synthesizer emulating the Roland JUNO-6. Digitally controlled oscillators (pulse, saw, square) with a sub-oscillator for low-end weight, and a built-in vintage chorus (modes I and II) for instant width.

name: Ogun
description: An additive synthesizer with a massive engine stacking thousands of sine harmonics (up to 32,767). Harmonic mapping draws custom spectral curves to define timbre, and a separate impulse EQ shapes the harmonic content of the note's attack.

name: SimSynth Live
description: A subtractive synthesizer with a Warm mode that doubles oscillators for instant detuned thickness, a state-variable filter mixing low/band/high-pass, and an LFO routable to pitch, filter or pulse width.

name: MiniSynth
description: A compact subtractive synth built around a multi-mode oscillator that reconfigures for saw, pulse, bell and supersaw, a single Modifier knob giving per-waveform shaping (PWM, detune, pitch), and integrated delay, chorus, phaser and distortion.

name: Transistor Bass
description: A monophonic bass synth emulating the Roland TB-303. It morphs smoothly between square and sawtooth, includes an integrated 128-program sequencer holding both synth settings and patterns, and a Rat-style overdrive for acid grit.

name: Wasp
description: A compact subtractive synthesizer with dual ADSR envelopes (amp and filter), LFO modulation routable to pitch and filter, and a built-in distortion for extra grit.

name: FL Keys
description: A sample-based keyboard rompler covering pianos, e-pianos and organs. It adds realism controls (key-release noise, hammer/pedal artifacts), velocity-sensitive samples for dynamics, and built-in overdrive and tremolo for Rhodes-style tones.

name: FL Slayer
description: A guitar physical-modelling instrument and amp simulator. It models string vibration and pickup harmonics, includes integrated amp and cabinet distortion, and offers specialized playing modes (autochords, strumming, power chords, glissando).

name: Plucked!
description: A physical-modelling synthesizer using Karplus-Strong synthesis: a delay line excited to produce string vibrations. Decay sets how long the string rings, and Color adjusts damping from nylon-like to steel-like character.

name: Fruity DrumSynth Live
description: A percussion synthesizer with dual noise/oscillator engines for thump and sizzle, a morphing bend-wheel to move between two drum patches, and envelope retriggering to build claps and multi-hit effects.

name: Drumaxx
description: A drum-machine instrument built on modelled percussion synthesis, providing tunable, editable drum voices across pads rather than fixed samples.

name: Hardcore
description: A guitar amp simulator and multi-effects rack. It offers five cabinet models for air and resonance, a drag-and-drop pedal chain to reorder effects, and a standalone mode for live use.

name: Frequency Shifter
description: A frequency shifter and ring modulator. Unlike a pitch shifter it moves every frequency by a fixed number of Hz (linearly), breaking harmonic relationships for inharmonic, metallic and bell-like tones. Ring modulation, independent stereo detuning and a feedback loop extend the effect.

name: Pitcher
description: A real-time pitch-correction and harmonizer (autotune). It snaps input pitch to the nearest note of a chosen scale, or follows notes played live on a MIDI keyboard, with control over correction speed and formants for natural or hard-tuned effects.

name: Effector
description: A performance-oriented multi-effect offering a set of switchable effects, each mapped to an X/Y pad for two-parameter control, with a built-in LFO and a dry/wet lever for momentary engagement - designed for live switching and automation.

name: Transient Processor
description: A transient shaper that boosts or cuts the attack and the release (sustain) of a signal independently of its level (threshold-independent). A split-frequency option keeps lows out of the processing path, and a drive control saturates the shaped transients.

name: Autogun
description: A free preset-exploration synthesizer built on the Ogun additive engine. Rather than editing parameters directly, you browse an enormous numbered preset space to discover new sounds.

name: Distructor
description: A multi-stage distortion and saturation effect offering several distortion and filtering stages for shaping grit, harmonics and tone.

name: Low Lifter
description: A psychoacoustic bass processor that synthesises upper harmonics from sub frequencies so low end reads clearly on small speakers, deepening perceived bass without simply boosting level.

name: LuxeVerb
description: An advanced algorithmic reverb with six specialised modes (Room, Plate, Hall, Cathedral, Space and Lush) and pitch-shifted feedback for shimmer and harmonic reverb tails.

name: Newtime
description: A time-stretching and pitch-shifting editor that changes audio length without altering pitch, or pitch without altering tempo, for warping and re-timing recorded audio.

name: Spreader
description: A psychoacoustic stereo widener that expands the stereo image while guaranteeing mono compatibility, so widened material does not vanish when summed to mono.

name: EQUO
description: A morphing graphic equalizer. You draw several EQ curves into banks and morph between them via LFO, automation or manual control, turning EQ into a movement and modulation effect.

name: Wasp XT
description: An enhanced version of the Wasp subtractive synthesizer with a clearer layout, more controls, and a modulation matrix routing LFOs and envelopes to pitch, pulse-width and cutoff.

name: Fruity Spectroman
description: A spectrum analyzer with a spectrograph mode (vertical peak bars across frequency) and a sonograph mode (a scrolling heatmap of frequency over time), for visual mixing and diagnosis.

name: Fruity Scratcher
description: A turntable emulator and sample manipulator. A virtual platter lets you click and drag the record or waveform to scratch, with an acceleration control for how quickly it returns to normal speed - for DJ-style scratches and tape stops.

name: DirectWave Sampler
description: An advanced multi-timbral sampling engine that imports and plays sampled instruments across key and velocity zones, with per-zone filters, envelopes and modulation, and support for common sampler formats.

name: Edison
description: An integrated audio editor and recorder that captures audio from any point in the mixer and provides waveform editing - trim, normalize, and spectral tools - directly inside the DAW.

name: SoundFont Player
description: A player for SoundFont (.sf2) instruments with a preset browser to access the multiple instruments inside a bank, plus envelope and LFO overrides to customise the built-in parameters.

name: Speech Synthesizer
description: A text-to-speech generator that turns any phrase into an audible speech sample, with a choice of voice personalities (such as Classic and Giant) for robotic and character vocals.

name: Drumpad
description: A pad-based drum sampler with an MPC-style layout for triggering drum samples, aimed at quick fingerdrumming and touch performance.

name: Emphasis
description: A dynamic enhancer and exciter that boosts low-level detail and excites transients while raising RMS loudness, keeping punch intact as the signal gets louder.

name: Emphasizer
description: A harmonic enhancement and excitement processor that adds character and perceived presence to a signal through added harmonic content.

name: Frequency Splitter
description: A crossover tool that splits a signal into frequency bands routed to separate paths, enabling multiband processing such as mono bass with widened highs.

name: Fruity Delay Bank
description: A multi-tap delay and filter bank with complex routing (a bank can feed the next bank or the main output) and grain controls that chop echoes into textures for evolving, granular delays.

name: Fruity LSD
description: A plugin that routes MIDI to the system's General MIDI synthesizer, playing the 128 standard GM sounds and custom DLS sound banks.

name: Wave Traveller
description: A scratch generator and sampler with a spline path editor for drawing playback speed and direction curves, with different scratch patterns mapped to MIDI notes.

name: Fruity Peak Controller
description: An internal controller that converts a source's audio level into an automation signal (peak follower) and also generates LFOs (sine, triangle, square, saw, random), for sidechain-style modulation of any linked parameter.

name: Fruity Envelope Controller
description: An internal controller that generates MIDI-triggered envelopes and LFOs, plus key, velocity and randomization sources, to modulate parameters in time with notes.

name: Fruity Formula Controller
description: An internal controller with a math engine (sin, cos, tan, log, abs, round, random and more) that computes an automation value from up to three linkable inputs, for logic- and formula-driven modulation.

name: Fruity Keyboard Controller
description: An internal controller that maps automation values to individual keyboard notes and to velocity, so which note is played (and how hard) drives effect parameters.

name: Fruity X-Y Controller
description: An internal vector controller providing X and Y automation outputs from a 2D pad, with smoothing for organic transitions between the mapped parameters.

name: Wave Candy
description: A metering and analysis plugin combining a zoomable oscilloscope, a linear/log spectrum analyzer and other visualizers for monitoring signals at any point in the mixer.

name: Fruity dB Meter
description: A level meter with a large peak display and a digital readout of exact dB values on hover, for monitoring loudness at any mixer point.

name: Fruity Center
description: A zero-latency utility that removes DC offset by re-centering the waveform vertically, cleaning up signals before further processing.

name: Fruity Send
description: A routing utility that extracts a signal from a mixer slot - optionally pre-fader - and sends it elsewhere while later slots keep processing, enabling flexible parallel routing.

name: Fruity PanOMatic
description: An auto-panner and volume LFO that automatically moves sound left/right (auto-pan) and modulates volume up/down (tremolo), with tempo-sync options.

name: Fruity Mute 2
description: A click-free mute utility with smooth on/off handling, placeable before effects (stopping the input) or after (stopping the output) for clean gating and routing.

name: SynthMaker
description: A visual programming environment for building custom instruments and effects from modular DSP blocks, wiring low-level signal-processing chains graphically.

name: Control Surface
description: A plugin providing a customizable panel of knobs, sliders and switches that can be linked to and automate other parameters, acting as a central control hub for a project.

name: Dashboard
description: A plugin for designing custom control-panel layouts and mapping their knobs and sliders to MIDI hardware, used to build tailored performance and automation surfaces.
